paragraphs:
This is an ordinary prose paragraph, long enough to be scored, sitting under a
heading. It has two lines and no protected content at all.

A second paragraph follows after a blank line. It mentions `inline code` and
$e^{i\pi}$ inline math, both of which stay inside the paragraph.

Prose immediately after the fence, with a blank line before it.
This paragraph runs right up to a code fence with no blank line between them:

- a list item paragraph survives segmentation (list handling is a scoring-policy
  question, not a segmentation one)
- with two items
The final paragraph closes the file, checking the end-of-document flush path.
